message:
fix(web): prevent renderer recreation on scroll with stable callbacks

Root cause: SequenceView passed inline arrow functions to useSequenceGrid's
onVisibleRangeChange and onZoomChange options. These inline functions are
recreated on every React render, which:

1. Changes the onZoomChange reference
2. handleZoomChange (which depends on onZoomChange) is recreated
3. The renderer creation effect re-runs (handleZoomChange is a dependency)
4. Renderer is destroyed and recreated

When scrolling, onVisibleRangeChange updates scroll position state, triggering
a React re-render, which recreates the renderer, which breaks scrolling.

Fix: Use useCallback with stable dependencies (zustand setters are stable)
to create stable callback references that don't trigger renderer recreation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
